mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds a high threshold, it signals a restrictive monetary policy stance that increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS value is 4.29, indicating a restrictive policy environment.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 4.0% over a 20-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate is a primary indicator of a weakening economy. This reduces aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, causing a decline in equity prices.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in UNRATE fails to show a statistically significant negative rank correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high interest rate environment, continued strength in retail sales signals persistent consumer demand that may fuel inflation. This forces the central bank to maintain a hawkish stance for longer, which is negative for equities.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically significantly negative compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production reflects a robust and expanding supply side of the economy. This translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, supporting positive equity market returns.
failure_condition: The 6-month momentum of INDPRO has a rolling 3-year rank correlation with 20-day forward returns that is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During a period of high inflation, an unexpectedly strong nonfarm payrolls report signals a tight labor market that could lead to wage-price spirals. This increases the probability of further monetary tightening, causing a negative short-term reaction in equity markets.
failure_condition: In high inflation regimes, days following strong PAYEMS reports do not show statistically significant negative 5-day returns compared to days following weak reports.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp year-over-year contraction in housing starts is a strong leading indicator of a broad economic downturn, as it reflects falling consumer confidence and investment. This portends lower future growth and negatively impacts equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a highly cyclical, interest-rate sensitive sector.
failure_condition: Following a trigger event, the S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation, as measured by the Consumer Price Index, erodes the real value of future earnings and increases the likelihood of restrictive monetary policy. Both factors put downward pressure on stock prices.
failure_condition: The 6-month change in CPIAUCSL fails to exhibit a statistically significant negative correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the year-over-year growth rate of real GDP below a critical threshold indicates a significant economic slowdown. This directly implies lower aggregate corporate earnings growth, leading to negative returns for the broad equity market.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 is the broadest measure of economic health.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically lower than in periods when the condition is not met.
